spec.item: A1-derive — Deterministic corner_map derive from single-corner result rows
spec.approach: The corner-on-map display is fully wired; the only gap is that `insight.corner_map` is set solely on the LLM synthesis path (via `applyInsightFields` reading `fields.corner_map`, mapInsight.ts:78). Add a deterministic `applyCornerMap(insight)` to web/src/lib/mapInsight.ts that mirrors `applyScalarHero`: it inspects `insight.rows`, and if (and only if) the rows carry a single distinct `corner_number` (numeric) — or, as a fallback, a single distinct non-empty `corner_label` — together with a `circuit_short_name`, it sets `insight.corner_map = { circuit, corner_number?, corner_label? }`. It bails out (returns unchanged) when `corner_map` already exists (LLM path wins), when there are no rows, when there is no `circuit_short_name`, or when there is more than one distinct corner (multi-corner grouped-bar shape) or no corner column at all (non-corner results). Then wire it into the client finalization pipeline in web/src/app/page.tsx right after `applyScalarHero`, and add the import. This runs on both the deterministic and LLM final-fold paths (page.tsx lines 273-277), and because it no-ops when corner_map is already present, it never clobbers an LLM-emitted corner_map.
spec.verification_prompt: Live chat prompt that yields a single-corner metric card: \"What was the average entry, apex, and exit speed through Turn 22 at Jeddah in 2025 qualifying?\" — expect the card to render the CornerMiniMap (T22 highlighted on the Jeddah outline) even though the LLM emitted no corner_map. Then a NEGATIVE check: \"Compare Verstappen and Leclerc entry speeds through every corner at Silverstone\" (multi-corner grouped bar) must NOT show a corner mini-map. Unit-side: add cases to web/scripts/tests/mapInsight.test.ts asserting applyCornerMap sets corner_map for rows=[{circuit_short_name:'Jeddah',corner_number:22,corner_label:'Turn 22',entry_speed_kph:250}] and leaves it undefined for two distinct corner_number rows and for rows with no corner column; run `tsx --test web/scripts/tests/mapInsight.test.ts`.
spec.contract_risks: None to ChartSeries fields, two-layer color tokens, or the honesty UI — this only populates the optional `corner_map` field already defined on InsightMock/DraftInsight (chart-types.ts:223) and already consumed downstream (toCardProps.ts:72 → insight-card.tsx → CornerMiniMap). Typecheck-safe: applyCornerMap is a pure DraftInsight→DraftInsight function matching the existing apply* signatures, uses only fields already on DraftInsight, and returns the same shape the anthropic validator produces (circuit/corner_number/corner_label). The no-overwrite guard (`if (insight.corner_map) return insight`) preserves the LLM path so no regression to synthesis-emitted corner_map. Corner-label trimming/slicing is looser than anthropic.ts's 40-char slice but harmless for the client-side outline lookup which matches case-insensitively.
spec.routing_note: No detector or router change needed — corner_map is a card SLOT field (like at_a_glance/hero), not a detector-routed chart shape. It is threaded from DraftInsight through toCardProps (cornerMap) into <InsightCard>, which renders <CornerMiniMap> when cornerMap is present. The single behavioral condition is that the derive runs in the page.tsx final fold; placing applyCornerMap after applyScalarHero (both operate on insight.rows) guarantees rows are populated (folded from the table part at line 268) before it runs.

verify.item: A1-derive — Deterministic corner_map derive from single-corner result rows
findings: Not a defect, but a documented over-fire edge: applyCornerMap does NOT check insight.chart, so a single-corner query that also carries driver_name (e.g. 'Verstappen's entry/apex/exit speed through Turn 22') routes to the grouped_bar detector (registry.ts:161 returns true on corner_label + abs-speed col + driver_name) AND gets a corner_map. insight-card.tsx renders cornerMap (line 185) and chart (line 196) independently, so the card shows a 1-group bar chart AND the mini-map together. The corner_map is still accurate (single distinct corner), so this is cosmetic, not a correctness/contract break — but the spec's stated intent ('single-corner metric card') silently also applies to single-corner charts. The verification prompt's positive case (aggregate, no driver_name) avoids this since grouped_bar's matches() returns false without driver_name or >=2 wide prefixes.

Minor verification-command path nuance: spec says `tsx --test web/scripts/tests/mapInsight.test.ts` but the test header documents running from web/ as `tsx --test scripts/tests/mapInsight.test.ts`. The file is .ts (confirmed) and tsx is the correct runner; just adjust cwd/path.

spec.approach: Extend the TimelineEvent contract with three optional, data-gated fields (corner_label, corner_f, circuit) and have the eventTimelineDetector populate them ONLY when the race-control message_text yields a corner reference (regex over the message) and the row carries circuit_short_name — never invented. Add circuit_short_name to the race-control-incidents SQL sess CTE so the row supplies the circuit. Rebuild timeline-chart.tsx as per-driver horizontal lanes over a shared lap ruler with kind-based icons, and when any event has a resolved corner render a small TrackMap pin below that lane by reusing the exact CornerMiniMap resolution pattern (resolve corner number/label against outline.corners, place a markers=[{f,color}] pin). The corner f-fraction resolves client-side from the track-outline API — no corner_analysis join needed. Both type:"timeline" (mock) and type:"event_timeline" (detector) route to this same component, so the rebuild must render with events that have none of the new fields.

files.detail: The insight builder reads rows[0].location / country_name / year / session_name. It does NOT read circuit, so no change is strictly required here. NO EDIT to this file — left as-is. (Listed only to confirm it was checked; do not modify.)

Add circuit_short_name to the session CTE and project it so the event row can drive the on-track pin. Two edits.

EDIT 1 — add circuit_short_name to the sess CTE. OLD:

    sess AS (
      SELECT country_name, location, year, session_name
      FROM core.sessions
      WHERE session_key = ${targetSession}
      LIMIT 1
    )

NEW:

    sess AS (
      SELECT country_name, location, circuit_short_name, year, session_name
      FROM core.sessions
      WHERE session_key = ${targetSession}
      LIMIT 1
    )

EDIT 2 — project circuit_short_name in the final SELECT (insert the sub-select right after the location line). OLD:

      (SELECT country_name FROM sess) AS country_name,
      (SELECT location FROM sess) AS location,
      (SELECT year FROM sess) AS year,
      (SELECT session_name FROM sess) AS session_name

NEW:

      (SELECT country_name FROM sess) AS country_name,
      (SELECT location FROM sess) AS location,
      (SELECT circuit_short_name FROM sess) AS circuit_short_name,
      (SELECT year FROM sess) AS year,
      (SELECT session_name FROM sess) AS session_name

spec.routing_note: Shape routes correctly with no registry-array change. The eventTimelineDetector already exists at priority 92 (matches lap+kind+driver — the exact columns the race-control SQL projects) and is registered in CHART_DETECTORS (line 1629). It emits type:\"event_timeline\", which index.tsx line 85-86 already routes to TimelineChart. The mock fixture uses type:\"timeline\" which routes via line 51-52 to the SAME component. Both paths hit the rebuilt component. The new corner/circuit fields are additive on TimelineEvent so no other consumer breaks (grep confirms TimelineEvent is consumed only by timeline-chart.tsx). The pin only appears when the SQL row carries circuit_short_name AND the message names a corner — otherwise the lanes render alone.
spec.contract_risks: Low. (1) TimelineEvent gains only optional fields — back-compat, no ChartSeries change. (2) Two-layer color rule preserved: e.team_color is a raw domain hex from the detector's getTeamColor (domain layer), and the pin/ruler chrome uses theme tokens hsl(var(--primary)), bg-chart-axis, text-section-label, bg-surface-raised, text-red-text (theme layer). (3) Honesty UI preserved: the pin returns null when the outline or corner can't be resolved and the corner is parsed strictly from message_text (data-gated, never invented); adding circuit_short_name to the SQL does not alter the no-data/table/reasoning paths. (4) Typecheck: chart.circuit is an existing ChartSpec field; TrackMap markers/highlights/variant props all pre-exist. Verify the popover/popover-foreground Tailwind tokens exist in globals.css/tailwind config — if not, swap to bg-surface-raised text-foreground (both confirmed in use). One thing to double-check on apply: whether core.sessions exposes circuit_short_name in the same casing (the track-outline route queries core.sessions.circuit_short_name, so it does).
spec.verification_prompt: Live chat: \"Show the steward penalties and incidents at the 2025 São Paulo Grand Prix\" (triggers INCIDENT_TRIGGER → session_race_control_incidents template → event_timeline detector). Expect per-driver lanes over a lap ruler with kind icons; if any steward message names a corner (e.g. \"AT TURN 4\") and circuit_short_name resolves in track-outline, an on-track pin renders below. Also re-open the /mock m15 fixture (type:\"timeline\", no corner/circuit) to confirm the lanes still render with no pin (back-compat).

spec.approach: Extend the deterministic SQL (telemetryWeatherGap.ts) to also emit circuit_short_name, a derived round (DENSE_RANK over meetings by date within the year), and location per row — keeping the existing per-session columns (session_label, telemetry, weather, year, location, session_name_raw) so the existing insight builder and the status_grid detector's matches() are unchanged. In the status_grid detector's build(), roll the per-session rows up per venue (green=no gaps, red=all sessions gap, amber otherwise) and emit a new venues[] array + venue_grid:true on the same status_grid ChartSpec (legacy table rows kept for back-compat). Replace StatusGridChart's render: when venue_grid is set, render a CSS grid of a memoized per-venue child component that calls useTrackOutline(circuit) (so the 24 /api/track-outline fetches are one-per-child, cached by the hook), tinting a single full-lap TrackMap variant="mini" segment by status with an Rn round label; otherwise fall back to the existing table. Keep an all-clear empty state when every venue is green.

files.detail:
REPLACE the whole SQL string (the `const sql = \`...\`` block, lines 38-64) so it derives round + carries circuit_short_name/location per row while preserving every existing output column. The gate/trigger code above it is unchanged.

OLD (verbatim, lines 38-64):
  const sql = `
    WITH tele AS (
      -- Telemetry presence via the per-driver coverage rollup, NOT
      -- raw.car_data — an unfiltered DISTINCT over the raw telemetry
      -- table is a full scan that blows the query timeout.
      SELECT DISTINCT session_key
      FROM analytics.telemetry_coverage_per_driver
      WHERE car_data_samples > 0
    ),
    wx AS (
      SELECT DISTINCT session_key FROM raw.weather
    )
    SELECT
      s.session_key,
      s.session_name || ' · ' || s.location || ' ' || s.year AS session_label,
      'full' AS telemetry,
      CASE WHEN w.session_key IS NULL THEN 'missing' ELSE 'full' END AS weather,
      s.year,
      s.location,
      s.session_name AS session_name_raw
    FROM raw.sessions s
    JOIN tele t ON t.session_key = s.session_key
    LEFT JOIN wx w ON w.session_key = s.session_key
    WHERE TRUE ${yearFilter}
    ORDER BY CASE WHEN w.session_key IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, s.date_start
    LIMIT 30
  `;

NEW (verbatim):
  const sql = `
    WITH tele AS (
      -- Telemetry presence via the per-driver coverage rollup, NOT
      -- raw.car_data — an unfiltered DISTINCT over the raw telemetry
      -- table is a full scan that blows the query timeout.
      SELECT DISTINCT session_key
      FROM analytics.telemetry_coverage_per_driver
      WHERE car_data_samples > 0
    ),
    wx AS (
      SELECT DISTINCT session_key FROM raw.weather
    ),
    rounds AS (
      -- No round column exists on raw.meetings; derive it as the meeting's
      -- chronological rank within its season so the venue grid can label
      -- each circuit R1..R24.
      SELECT
        meeting_key,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY year ORDER BY date_start, meeting_key) AS round
      FROM raw.meetings
    )
    SELECT
      s.session_key,
      s.session_name || ' · ' || s.location || ' ' || s.year AS session_label,
      'full' AS telemetry,
      CASE WHEN w.session_key IS NULL THEN 'missing' ELSE 'full' END AS weather,
      s.year,
      s.location,
      s.circuit_short_name,
      r.round,
      s.session_name AS session_name_raw
    FROM raw.sessions s
    JOIN tele t ON t.session_key = s.session_key
    LEFT JOIN wx w ON w.session_key = s.session_key
    LEFT JOIN rounds r ON r.meeting_key = s.meeting_key
    WHERE TRUE ${yearFilter}
    ORDER BY CASE WHEN w.session_key IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, s.date_start
    LIMIT 200
  `;

NOTE: LIMIT raised 30→200 because the grid rolls up per venue; 24 venues × ~5 sessions/venue needs the full season, not the first 30 rows. querySafety is satisfied — no banned keyword (DENSE_RANK/OVER/PARTITION allowed; the banned word is "analyze", not "analytics"). raw.sessions has meeting_key+circuit_short_name and raw.meetings has meeting_key+year+date_start (confirmed in sql/002_create_tables.sql), so the join columns all exist.

ALSO update the doc comment (lines 8-14). OLD:
 * One row per session that has telemetry, with full/missing status
 * columns shaped for the status_grid detector (a column whose values are
 * full/partial/missing plus a session label). Sessions missing weather
 * sort first; the insight builder reads the per-row statuses to report
 * the gap count — including the "0 gaps" case, which previously rendered
 * as a bare "No rows matched" instead of the actual answer ("full
 * coverage").
NEW:
 * One row per session that has telemetry, with full/missing status
 * columns shaped for the status_grid detector (a column whose values are
 * full/partial/missing plus a session label). Each row also carries
 * circuit_short_name + a derived season round so the status_grid detector
 * can roll the sessions up into a per-VENUE coverage grid (24 mini
 * circuit outlines). Sessions missing weather sort first; the insight
 * builder reads the per-row statuses to report the gap count — including
 * the "0 gaps" case, which previously rendered as a bare "No rows
 * matched" instead of the actual answer ("full coverage").

spec.verification_prompt: Live chat prompt through the real UI: "Which 2025 sessions have telemetry but no matching weather data?" — hits TRIGGER_TELEMETRY + TRIGGER_WEATHER + TRIGGER_GAP + the 2025 year filter, routing to sessions_telemetry_without_weather → status_grid detector → venue_grid render. Confirm a grid of ~24 mini circuit outlines (each with an Rn label + venue name), tinted green/amber/red, with the all-clear banner when zero gaps. Then run `npm run typecheck` plus `node scripts/tests/mapInsight.test.ts` (and the status_grid/answer-cache tests) to confirm the legacy table path and telemetryWeatherGapInsight builder still pass.
spec.contract_risks: ChartSeries fields untouched. Two-layer tokens respected: status tints use THEME tokens hsl(var(--semantic-positive/negative), --accent-amber) (status is not a team/compound domain color); tile chrome uses --surface-raised/--section-label/--border. Honesty UI preserved: telemetryWeatherGapInsight.ts still receives telemetry/weather/session_label per row unchanged, so gap-count and full-coverage prose + SQL/table/reasoning panels are intact. Typecheck risk low/localized: new optional ChartSpec fields (venue_grid, venues) + VenueCoverage are additive. Watch item: SQL LIMIT raised 30→200 to fetch the full season for the rollup — grep found no test asserting exactly 30 rows; the builder uses rows.length and only renders \"30+\" formatting, still correct. Derived round via DENSE_RANK is a chronological heuristic, not an official round number, but adequate for the tile label. querySafety satisfied (no banned keyword; \"analytics\" is not the banned word \"analyze\").

spec.approach: Add a new deterministic template `driver_pair_corner_delta` (new file `web/src/lib/deterministicSql/cornerDelta.ts`) returning ONE row per corner in `analytics.corner_analysis` with each driver's best entry/apex/exit speed, the three signed phase deltas (A−B), the corner window `zone_f0/zone_f1`, and a unique marker column `corner_delta_kind`. A new high-priority detector `cornerDeltaGridDetector` (priority 108, matches on `corner_delta_kind`) builds a `corner_delta_grid` ChartSpec with per-corner tile data + track-map node markers (sized by |apex delta|, colored by faster driver) + a diverging apex-delta ladder. A new card `<CornerDeltaGrid>` reuses `<TrackMap markers=…>` and `<DivergingBarChart>` plus phase tiles. A deterministic insight builder `buildCornerDeltaInsight` supplies the honesty card, wired in orchestration by templateKey. Separately baseline the generic `GroupedBarChart` y-axis at 0 for absolute-speed (large-range) data so residual grouped_bar comparisons stop exaggerating small gaps.
spec.routing_note: Routing works because chart detection (runDetectorRegistry) runs on the SQL result COLUMN SHAPE regardless of template origin, and detectors are sorted priority DESC (registry.ts line 1658). The new detector is priority 108 (above current max 107 telemetryOverlay) and matches ONLY on unique marker column `corner_delta_kind`, so it wins before groupedBarDetector(100)/brakeZoneDeltaDetector(101) could grab the a_/b_ absolute-speed columns. The template is reached in deterministicSql.ts placed AFTER the driver-pair session gate + brakeZones and BEFORE buildSectorDominanceTemplate (which today rejects entry/apex/exit and `turns 7,8,9` phrasings, sending them to LLM — exactly what this card should own). Topic guard `driver_pair_corner_delta: { owns: ["corner"] }` passes because CORNER_KEYWORDS include corner/apex/entry speed/exit speed. The insight card comes from orchestration's templateKey switch (new buildCornerDeltaInsight branch).

files.detail:
FULL NEW FILE: "use client" import type { ChartSpec } from "@/lib/chart-types" import { DivergingBarChart } from "./diverging-bar-chart" import { TrackMap, useTrackOutline } from "./track-map" /** * All-corner 2-driver entry/apex/exit delta card (A5). Three surfaces: * 1. Mini track map with a sized node at every corner (radius scaled by * |apex delta|, colored by the faster driver). * 2. Per-corner phase tiles (entry / apex / exit signed km/h deltas). * 3. Who-is-faster-where diverging ladder (apex delta per corner, biggest * gap first) via . * * Falls back to tiles + ladder when the circuit has no derived outline. */ export function CornerDeltaGrid({ chart }: { chart: ChartSpec }) { const outline = useTrackOutline(chart.circuit) const deltas = chart.corner_deltas ?? [] const drivers = chart.corner_delta_drivers const fmt = (v: number) => `${v > 0 ? "+" : ""}${v.toFixed(1)}` return (
{outline && deltas.length > 0 && ( ({ f: d.f, color: d.color, r: d.node_r }))} /> )} {drivers && (
{drivers.a} faster
{drivers.b} faster
)} {deltas.length > 0 && (
{deltas.map((d) => (
{d.label}
Entry
{fmt(d.entry_delta)}
Apex
{fmt(d.apex_delta)}
Exit
{fmt(d.exit_delta)}
))}
)}
) } NOTE: --surface-raised / --section-label / --border are tokens named in the task brief. Delta convention: positive = driver A carried more speed (A - B), matching ladder + insight. If `tabular-nums` is not in this Tailwind config, remove it (cosmetic).

EDIT 1 — import after TrackCornerDelta. OLD:

import { TrackCornerDelta } from "./track-corner-delta"
import { TrackSpeedMap } from "./track-speed-map"

NEW:

import { TrackCornerDelta } from "./track-corner-delta"
import { CornerDeltaGrid } from "./corner-delta-grid"
import { TrackSpeedMap } from "./track-speed-map"

EDIT 2 — switch case after track_corner_delta. OLD:

    case "track_corner_delta":
      return <TrackCornerDelta chart={chart} />

NEW:

    case "track_corner_delta":
      return <TrackCornerDelta chart={chart} />
    case "corner_delta_grid":
      return <CornerDeltaGrid chart={chart} />

EDIT 3 — re-export at bottom after DeltaComparison. OLD:

export { DeltaComparison } from "./delta-comparison"

NEW:

export { DeltaComparison } from "./delta-comparison"
export { CornerDeltaGrid } from "./corner-delta-grid"

EDIT 1 — add the insight-builder import after the brakeZones import (line 46). OLD:

import { buildBrakeZonesInsight } from "@/lib/synthesis/brakeZonesInsight";
import { buildSectorDominanceInsight } from "@/lib/synthesis/sectorDominanceInsight";

NEW:

import { buildBrakeZonesInsight } from "@/lib/synthesis/brakeZonesInsight";
import { buildCornerDeltaInsight } from "@/lib/synthesis/cornerDeltaInsight";
import { buildSectorDominanceInsight } from "@/lib/synthesis/sectorDominanceInsight";

EDIT 2 — add the templateKey branch in the deterministicInsight ternary chain (orchestration.ts ~lines 1636-1639), right after the driver_pair_brake_zones branch. OLD:

                                  : selectedTemplateKey === "driver_pair_brake_zones"
                                    ? buildBrakeZonesInsight(result.rows)
                                    : selectedTemplateKey === "driver_pair_sector_dominance"
                                      ? buildSectorDominanceInsight(result.rows)

NEW:

                                  : selectedTemplateKey === "driver_pair_brake_zones"
                                    ? buildBrakeZonesInsight(result.rows)
                                    : selectedTemplateKey === "driver_pair_corner_delta"
                                      ? buildCornerDeltaInsight(result.rows)
                                    : selectedTemplateKey === "driver_pair_sector_dominance"
                                      ? buildSectorDominanceInsight(result.rows)

NOTE: TypeScript ignores the ragged indentation of the inserted branch — the two new lines slot into the existing `? :` chain without re-indenting the tail. Re-read the exact anchor at ~1636 before applying (it is a deeply nested ternary).

Add the new file to SCAN_FILES so the coverage test scans the new templateKey literal. OLD:

  "src/lib/deterministicSql/brakeZones.ts",
  "src/lib/deterministicSql/sectorDominance.ts",

NEW:

  "src/lib/deterministicSql/brakeZones.ts",
  "src/lib/deterministicSql/cornerDelta.ts",
  "src/lib/deterministicSql/sectorDominance.ts",

NOTE: This test file is already modified on this branch (gitStatus) — re-read before applying to confirm the brakeZones/sectorDominance pair is still the adjacent anchor.

spec.verification_prompt: Live chat prompt: "Compare Verstappen and Leclerc corner by corner at Abu Dhabi 2025 — where did each gain on entry, apex and exit through every turn?" Expected: routes to templateKey driver_pair_corner_delta (guarded by owns:["corner"]), renders a corner_delta_grid card (mini track map with per-corner nodes sized by apex delta + colored by faster driver, per-corner entry/apex/exit tiles, who-is-faster-where diverging ladder) plus a deterministic insight card (title "Corner-by-corner — Verstappen vs Leclerc · ...", verdict naming the apex-corner winner + biggest swing). Then run in web/: `npx tsc --noEmit` (types balance, new optional ChartSpec fields) and `node --test scripts/tests/template-router-topic-coverage.test.mjs` (new templateKey annotated). Also sanity-check a residual absolute-speed grouped_bar ("show Max and Charles apex speeds at the three heaviest brake zones") now baselines its y-axis at 0.
spec.contract_risks: Low. ChartSeries optional fields (strokeDasharray/strokeWidth/opacity/emphasis) untouched; new ChartSpec fields are all optional so no existing detector/renderer breaks. Two-layer color rule preserved: DOMAIN team colors via getDistinctTeamColors/getTeamColor raw hex on ChartSpec.color/diverging_colors; THEME tokens (--surface-raised/--section-label/--border) only for card chrome via hsl(var(--token)). Honesty UI preserved — card renders alongside reasoning trace/SQL/table; the deterministic insight states the best-per-phase basis and telemetry-sample caveat. Watch items: (1) orchestration.ts ternary insertion is a deeply nested chain — re-read the ~1636 anchor and confirm exact whitespace before applying; (2) confirm InsightFields.verdict allows free-form label + optional color (brakeZonesInsight uses the same pattern, so known-good); (3) coverage test file is already modified on this branch — re-read before editing SCAN_FILES; (4) typecheck must pass with the new corner_delta_grid ChartType + optional fields.

findings: TYPECHECK BREAK (P0): buildCornerDeltaInsight in the new web/src/lib/synthesis/cornerDeltaInsight.ts sets verdict.label to `"EVEN"` or `${overallLeader.toUpperCase()} EDGE`, but InsightFields["verdict"].label is the literal union `"YES" | "NO"` (web/src/lib/chatTypes.ts:111 AND web/src/components/f1-chat/insight-card.tsx:32). `npx tsc --noEmit` will fail. The spec's NOTE that 'brakeZonesInsight uses the same pattern, so known-good' is FALSE — brakeZonesInsight.ts only ever assigns label:"YES"/"NO" (a genuine yes/no foreshadow question). A5 is not a yes/no question, so the verdict slot is the wrong surface both by type and by intent (VerdictCard renders it as a big YES/NO bypass badge). FIX: drop `verdict` from the returned InsightFields and lead with `at_a_glance` (free-form string, exists on InsightFields) or fold the summary into key_takeaways/metrics.

MINOR (cosmetic, not a break): the ladder in the corner_delta_grid spec passes diverging_colors {positive:aColor, negative:bColor}, but DivergingBarChart (web/src/components/f1-chat/charts/diverging-bar-chart.tsx:31) colors each bar via getTeamColorByDriver(label) where label is a CORNER name (e.g. 'Turn 1'), NOT a driver — so per-bar team colors won't apply and bars fall back to a default hue. diverging_colors is only used as the domain fallback, not per-bar. The ladder will still render (signed values, +/- axis) but won't be team-colored as the spec narrative implies. Non-blocking; note for expectation-setting.

MINOR (global behavioral change): the grouped-bar-chart.tsx baseline-at-0 edit (domainMin: 0 for range>=10) affects EVERY residual grouped_bar with a large value range, not just absolute-speed charts. For most absolute magnitudes this is the honest choice, but any grouped_bar whose values legitimately sit far from 0 (large counts, etc.) will now waste vertical space. Acceptable per the anti-exaggeration rationale, but it is a global change beyond A5's own card — flag for regression-eyeballing residual grouped_bar cards.

spec.item: B1-fastest-lap — Fastest-lap diamond markers on the line chart
spec.approach: Use the component-compute approach (no plumbing / no detector / no SQL change), exactly as the item recommends. In web/src/components/f1-chat/charts/line-chart.tsx, for each series compute the index of the minimum finite value (fastest lap = lowest lap time) and render a Recharts ReferenceDot shaped as a diamond at x=index+1 (the chart is 1-indexed by lap), y=minValue, colored with the series' domain color. Gate the whole feature on chart.y_value_format === "lap_time_s" so it only fires for absolute-lap-time charts — for delta/gap charts (decimal_seconds) or unformatted numeric charts a "fastest lap" minimum is meaningless and must not be drawn. Import ReferenceDot from recharts (already an available export alongside the existing ReferenceLine import).
spec.verification_prompt: Fire a live multi-lap absolute-lap-time line-chart prompt through the real chat UI (per visual_qa_via_live_ui memory), e.g. \"Show Verstappen and Norris lap times across the 2025 Japanese Grand Prix\" — this routes to the line detector with y_value_format=\"lap_time_s\". Confirm a diamond appears on each driver's line at their lowest point (fastest lap), colored the team color with a light stroke. Then fire a delta/gap prompt (e.g. \"gap to leader per lap\") and confirm NO diamonds render (that routes to race_trace/decimal_seconds, not this gate). Alternatively /mock the m09 fixture, which is the line detector's fixture, to sanity-check rendering.
spec.contract_risks: No ChartSeries/ChartSpec field changes — pure component compute, so the two-layer token contract and typecheck are unaffected. Uses the series' existing domain color (raw hex via ChartSeries.color) for the diamond fill and a theme token (--surface-raised) for the outline stroke, honoring the two-layer color rule. Honesty UI (reasoning trace, SQL, table, no-data, clarification) is untouched. One minor typing note: Recharts' ReferenceDot `shape` render-prop is loosely typed; the inline `(props: { cx?: number; cy?: number })` signature with the null guard keeps it strict-mode-clean and avoids `any`. The gate on y_value_format===\"lap_time_s\" guarantees the marker never appears on delta/gap or unformatted charts where a minimum is not a fastest lap.
spec.routing_note: No routing change needed. The line detector at web/src/lib/mapInsight/detectors/registry.ts already sets type:\"line\" and y_value_format:\"lap_time_s\" for absolute-lap-time result shapes (both wide and long format paths, lines 429-433 and 461-465), which is exactly the case where a fastest-lap marker is meaningful. The <LineChart> renderer is the sole consumer of type:\"line\". Delta/gap line shapes get decimal_seconds (or route to race_trace), so the y_value_format gate naturally excludes them.

verify.item: B1-fastest-lap — Fastest-lap diamond markers on the line chart

spec.approach: The live bug is that when the LLM echoes the user's question back as `insight.title`, `applyInsightFields` (web/src/lib/mapInsight.ts:75) sets `next.title` to that echoed prompt BEFORE `applyQuestionTitle` runs; `applyQuestionTitle` (line 516) then early-returns because the title is truthy and not "Insight", so the self-titled prompt survives to render. The fix is entirely inside web/src/lib/mapInsight.ts and needs NO change to the page.tsx call site (page.tsx:277 already passes `folded`, which carries `rows`). I add (1) an `isSelfTitle(title, question)` normalized comparator, (2) a `titleFromRows(rows)` deterministic deriver that assembles a clean title from resolved references present in the result rows (distinct driver surnames + venue `circuit_short_name` + `corner_label` + a metric/topic hint), and (3) a guard in `applyQuestionTitle` that DROPS a self-title, then prefers `titleFromRows` and falls back to the existing filler-stripping `titleFromQuestion` (which never echoes the verbatim prompt because it strips leading question filler and cuts at the sentence break). This also covers the reported 0-row scatter/corner case: with empty rows `titleFromRows` returns undefined and the cleaned `titleFromQuestion` output is used instead of the raw prompt.

files.detail: See files array above for the three exact old→new edits (self-title guard + titleFromRows deriver inserted before titleFromQuestion at line 494; applyQuestionTitle rewrite at line 515; __test export extension at line 521).

spec.verification_prompt: Two-part verification. (1) Unit: add a node test (or run in a REPL) exercising the new __test exports — assert isSelfTitle('Entry, apex, and exit speeds for Hamilton through Turn 3 at Silverstone 2025', 'entry, apex and exit speeds for Hamilton through Turn 3 at Silverstone 2025') === true; assert titleFromRows([{driver_name:'Lewis HAMILTON', circuit_short_name:'Silverstone', corner_label:'Turn 3'}], 'apex speed for Hamilton at Silverstone') === 'Apex Speed: HAMILTON — Silverstone Turn 3'; assert titleFromRows([], 'any question') === undefined (0-row falls back to titleFromQuestion). (2) Live: preview_start name 'web' (port 3000), fire through the real chat UI the confirmed-bug prompt 'entry, apex and exit speeds for Verstappen through Turn 3 at Silverstone 2025' (a query that returns 0 or few rows) and confirm the card header is NOT the raw prompt verbatim but a cleaned/derived title. Also run 'cd web && npx tsc --noEmit' — must stay green.
spec.contract_risks: None to the load-bearing contracts. No ChartSeries fields touched, no color tokens touched, no detector/SQL/honesty-UI changes. `titleFromRows` reads only optional row fields (`driver_name`/`circuit_short_name`/`corner_label`) defensively via String() with `?? \"\"`, so malformed/absent columns yield undefined, never a throw. `applyQuestionTitle` keeps its existing signature and call site (page.tsx:277) unchanged, so no caller update is needed. Typecheck risk is nil (pure additions to an already-clean file). The only behavioral change is that an echoed-question title is now replaced — a strict improvement; the `q.length >= 12` substring guard in isSelfTitle prevents a legitimately short, distinct LLM title from being mistaken for an echo.
spec.routing_note: Not applicable to routing — title is a slot field resolved on the client fold path (page.tsx applyQuestionTitle), not detector-routed. No shape/detector/template is involved; this changes only which string lands in insight.title before render by insight-card.tsx.

findings: Missing edit strings: the spec's only files entry says 'See files array above for the three exact old→new edits' but NO actual old→new find/replace strings (nor full-file contents) are present in the payload. The task requires mechanically-applicable EXACT edits; as delivered this cannot be applied. The three edits (isSelfTitle + titleFromRows insertion before line 494, applyQuestionTitle rewrite at 515-518, __test extension at 521-528) are only described in prose, never given as literal strings.

Unverifiable unit assertion: titleFromRows([{driver_name:'Lewis HAMILTON', circuit_short_name:'Silverstone', corner_label:'Turn 3'}], 'apex speed for Hamilton at Silverstone') === 'Apex Speed: HAMILTON — Silverstone Turn 3' presumes surname extraction from 'Lewis HAMILTON', a title-cased metric/topic hint ('Apex Speed') derived from the QUESTION not the rows, and a specific ':'/'—' join scheme — none of which is specified. The exact-string assertion cannot be checked without the actual titleFromRows body.

Minor: isSelfTitle example compares title 'Entry, apex, and exit speeds...' (with comma) vs question 'entry, apex and exit speeds...' (no 'and' comma) — equality requires a punctuation-stripping normalizer that is asserted but not defined in the provided spec text.

spec.item: B17-clarification — Clarification choice card (sprint-weekend "which qualifying?" disambiguation)
spec.approach: Clarification is ALREADY surfaced only as plain prose today: when `runtime.resolution.needsClarification` is true, orchestration.ts returns `{answer: clarificationPrompt, generationSource:"runtime_clarification", runtime}` and the frontend folds `answer` into the insight body as text — the human-readable `runtime.resolution.sessionCandidates[]` (each has `sessionKey`, `sessionName`, `year`, `label`, `confidence`, `score`) are on the wire but never rendered as pickable options. The fix is pure frontend/typing: (1) declare `sessionCandidates` on `ChatApiResponse.runtime.resolution`; (2) add an optional `clarification` field to `DraftInsight`/`InsightMock`; (3) a new `applyClarification(insight, response, question)` mapper populates it from candidates when `generationSource==="runtime_clarification"`; (4) a new `<ClarificationCard>` renders human-readable option buttons (session type + venue/year label, NEVER a raw key in the button label); (5) InsightCard renders it and page.tsx wires each option's `onResolve` to re-send `"<originalQuestion> (session <sessionKey>)"` through the existing `handleSend`, which `parseSessionKeyMention` in chatRuntime already pins deterministically (bypassing clarification). No SQL, no detector, no orchestration/runtime logic change.

files.detail:
Forward the clarification field from the insight to the card props. OLD:
    cornerMap: m.corner_map,
    takeaways: m.key_takeaways,
NEW:
    cornerMap: m.corner_map,
    clarification: m.clarification
      ? { prompt: m.clarification.prompt, options: m.clarification.options }
      : undefined,
    takeaways: m.key_takeaways,

spec.verification_prompt: Live UI: submit a session-type-ambiguous sprint-weekend prompt with NO explicit session type or year, e.g. \"Who was fastest at Imola?\" or \"What was the fastest lap at the Miami sprint weekend?\" — the runtime's same-meeting close-score tie sets needsClarification with 2+ sessionCandidates (Practice/Qualifying/Sprint/Race of one meeting). Confirm the assistant renders the ClarificationCard with human-readable option buttons (session type bold + venue/year subline, no raw session_key visible), a highlighted \"Most likely\" primary, and that clicking one re-sends \"<original question> (session <key>)\" which resolves deterministically and returns a real answer card. Cross-check candidate labels against runtime.resolution.sessionCandidates in the network response body.
spec.routing_note: No detector/renderer routing involved — clarification is a SLOT-style branch, not a chart-detector shape. It routes purely on `generationSource === \"runtime_clarification\"` + presence of `runtime.resolution.sessionCandidates` (already emitted by orchestration.ts's needsClarification branch, which returns the full `runtime` object). The only reason candidates aren't usable today is that `ChatApiResponse.runtime.resolution` doesn't declare `sessionCandidates` — the data is already on the wire (confirmed: orchestration returns `runtime` whole; chatRuntime populates `resolution.sessionCandidates` with sessionKey/sessionName/year/label/confidence/score). The re-send routes back through the deterministic session-key pin because parseSessionKeyMention matches \"session <key>\".
spec.contract_risks: ChartSeries fields untouched. Two-layer color rule preserved: the card uses only THEME tokens (--primary, --red-text, --section-label, --surface-raised, --muted-foreground, --border) via hsl utility classes — no domain hex, no ChartSeries.color. Honesty UI preserved: reasoning trace, SQL, table, no-data, and driver-pair/prose clarifications are untouched (applyClarification early-returns unless generationSource===\"runtime_clarification\" AND candidates.length>=2, so \"specify the driver\"/\"specify the session\" prose clarifications keep their existing text-body rendering). Typecheck: new optional fields on InsightMock/DraftInsight are additive; toCardProps and InsightCard props are optional; applyClarification is a new named export. One thing to watch — `next.body = \"\"` in applyClarification runs AFTER applyResponseSemantics but BEFORE applyScalarHero/applyVerdictSemantics; those early-return when hero/verdict already set and won't fire on an empty body, and applyQuestionTitle still sets a title, so the card won't render blank (ClarificationCard is gated only on options, and InsightCard.hasContent isn't required for the clarification block since it renders above the hero unconditionally).

findings: No routing/typecheck/contract issues found — spec is applicable as written. Verified: orchestration.ts:698-711 returns the FULL `runtime` object on the runtime_clarification branch, and runtime.resolution.sessionCandidates (chatRuntime.ts:106, 2334) is populated with the exact fields the mapper reads — sessionKey, sessionName, year, confidence, score, label (buildSessionLabel in chatRuntime/resolution.ts:202 joins with ' / ', matching the mapper's split('/')). So the data really is on the wire; only the ChatApiResponse type omits sessionCandidates today (chatTypes.ts:136-144 has no such field). The frontend re-send path is sound: parseSessionKeyMention (chatRuntime.ts:694-695) uses /\bsession(?:\s+key)?\s*(\d{3,6})\b/i, which '(session <key>)' matches.

No SQL/matview/detector change needed — correctly claimed false. This is a SLOT-style branch keyed on generationSource==='runtime_clarification', not a column-shape detector, so registry.ts is untouched. applyClarification early-returns unless generationSource==='runtime_clarification' AND candidates.length>=2, preserving prose/driver-pair clarifications and the honesty UI.

All OLD anchor strings verified present and unique: chatTypes.ts:136-144 (resolution block), chart-types.ts:223 corner_map + interface close (DraftInsight extends Omit<InsightMock,'title'> at :237 so the field propagates), mapInsight.ts:157-162 (applyResponseSemantics close + applyScalarHero banner; ChatApiResponse/DraftInsight/generationSource already imported at :1-2/:136), insight-card.tsx:5/23-24/71-73/151-152 (all three edits), toCardProps.ts:72-73, page.tsx:16-23 import block, :273-275 fold sequence, :397 InsightCard render.

Render/order edge cases check out. body='' + chart=undefined set by applyClarification cause no blank card: applyScalarHero (mapInsight.ts:203-205) and applyVerdictSemantics early-return because a clarification response has no result/rows (mapChatResponse.ts:25 only emits a table part when data.result exists), applyResponseSemantics (mapInsight.ts:138) only acts on no_data_refusal/proprietary sources, applyQuestionTitle still sets a title, and hasContent (insight-card.tsx:91) only gates the streaming 'Thinking…' fallback (streaming is false post-fold), NOT CardContent — the clarification block at :151 renders unconditionally above the hero. The duplicated prose is correctly cleared by body=''.

Token/contract rules preserved. All utility classes used by ClarificationCard resolve: text-red-text/bg-surface-raised/text-section-label registered in tailwind.config.ts:43/45/48; primary/border/muted-foreground/foreground are standard tokens. No domain hex, no ChartSeries.color touched → two-layer color rule intact. cn exported from utils.ts:4. Typecheck additive: new fields optional; the two structurally-identical ClarificationOption declarations (clarification-card.tsx vs chart-types.ts) are assignable; 'label ?? [...].join(" ") ?? "session"' compiles (join always yields string) — only a harmless redundant-nullish lint smell, not an error. `text` is in scope in handleSend (page.tsx:101).

spec.item: B19-dead-renderers — wire-or-delete decision for delta_comparison, bare timeline, and donut
spec.approach: Three separate calls. (1) delta_comparison: DELETE — it is truly dead (only the ChartType member, the index.tsx switch case + comment, and the component/export reference it; no detector or fixture emits it). Remove the member, the switch case, both imports/exports, and the component file. (2) bare `timeline`: DELETE the ChartType member + switch case, and MIGRATE the one live consumer — the M15 `incidentsMock` fixture (_source.ts:206), whose file is already named m15-event-timeline.ts and whose manifest already declares chartType "event_timeline" — from `type: "timeline"` to `type: "event_timeline"`. Both types already render through the identical TimelineChart, so this is behavior-preserving and removes the mismatch. (3) donut: WIRE it by adding a deterministic `session_type_share` SQL template that aliases GROUP BY session_type to columns `label` + `session_count`, which exactly satisfies donutDetector.matches (has `label`, has a /count/ column, 2-6 rows, no `driver_name`). Register it in the router before the session gate (season-grain, no session pin) and mark its templateKey exempt from the topic guard (the guard comment reserves the exempt list for session-metadata templates that no topic owns).
spec.routing_note: donutDetector already exists (registry.ts:854, priority 70) and its matches() fires on rows with a `label` column + any column matching /value|count|share|pct|percent/ + row count 2-6 + no `driver_name`. It does NOT currently route in the LLM path because an LLM asked "how many sessions of each type" aliases the column `session_type` (not `label`) — so the shape never matches. The fix is a deterministic template that aliases the group key to `label` and the aggregate to `session_count` (contains "count"), guaranteeing the row shape the detector needs. A 2025 season has 5 distinct session_types in core.sessions (Practice/Qualifying/Race/Sprint/Sprint Qualifying → but note Practice 1/2/3 share session_type 'Practice'), landing safely in the 2-6 row window. delta_comparison and bare timeline are NOT detector-routed (delta_comparison is never emitted by any detector; timeline is only ever produced by the M15 fixture and the live event_timeline detector emits event_timeline), so deleting them changes no routing. The M15 migration keeps the same TimelineChart render because index.tsx already maps event_timeline → TimelineChart (line 85-86).
spec.contract_risks: No ChartSeries field changes. No two-layer token changes (donut supplies its own hex palette in its build; delta_comparison used the same convention). Honesty UI untouched. Typecheck risk if the `timeline`/`delta_comparison` ChartType members are removed while a consumer still references the literal — mitigated by (a) migrating the only `timeline` consumer (_source.ts:206) in the same change and (b) removing all delta_comparison references (chart-types member, index.tsx import+case+comment, index.tsx re-export, and the component file). web/scripts/health/a_surface_manifest.json still lists a `"timeline"` key (lines 726, 820) — it is a JSON data file, not typechecked, so it will not break the build; optionally retitle to event_timeline for consistency (non-blocking). classification.ts:82 `lower.includes("timeline")` matches question TEXT, not the ChartType, so it is unaffected.

files.detail:
Remove the two dead ChartType members. OLD:
  | "timeline"
  | "event_timeline"
NEW:
  | "event_timeline"

AND separately (this is the last union member, currently terminated by a semicolon on the delta_comparison line):
OLD:
  | "telemetry_overlay"
  | "delta_comparison";
NEW:
  | "telemetry_overlay";

Migrate the M15 incidentsMock fixture from the deleted bare `timeline` type to `event_timeline` (identical render path; aligns with manifest.ts:210 which already declares chartType "event_timeline"). This edit is uniquely anchored by the surrounding events array. OLD:
  chart: {
    type: "timeline",
    events: [
      { lap: 12, driver: "Sainz", kind: "track_limits", team_color: "#64C4FF", message: "5 SECOND TIME PENALTY — TRACK LIMITS" },
NEW:
  chart: {
    type: "event_timeline",
    events: [
      { lap: 12, driver: "Sainz", kind: "track_limits", team_color: "#64C4FF", message: "5 SECOND TIME PENALTY — TRACK LIMITS" },

Three edits in this file.

EDIT 1 — remove the delta-comparison import. OLD:
import { CompositeCard } from "./composite-card"
import { DeltaComparison } from "./delta-comparison"
import { TrackCornerDelta } from "./track-corner-delta"
NEW:
import { CompositeCard } from "./composite-card"
import { TrackCornerDelta } from "./track-corner-delta"

EDIT 2 — remove the dead delta_comparison switch case + comment (the `grouped_bar` case above and `line` case below anchor it uniquely). OLD:
    case "grouped_bar":
      return <GroupedBarChart chart={chart} className={className} />
    case "delta_comparison":
      return <DeltaComparison chart={chart} className={className} />
    // Backwards compat: nothing in the live pipeline emits "delta_comparison"

    case "line":
      return <LineChart chart={chart} className={className} />
NEW:

      return <GroupedBarChart chart={chart} className={className} />
    case "line":
      return <LineChart chart={chart} className={className} />

EDIT 3 — remove the delta-comparison re-export (last export in the file). OLD:
export { CompositeCard } from "./composite-card"
export { DeltaComparison } from "./delta-comparison"
NEW:

Note: the bare `timeline` switch case (`case "timeline": return <TimelineChart .../>`) is ALSO removed since the type is deleted. OLD:
    case "timeline":
      return <TimelineChart chart={chart} className={className} />
    case "radar":
NEW:
    case "radar":
The `case "event_timeline": return <TimelineChart .../>` branch (lines 85-86) is KEPT and now serves the migrated M15 fixture.

DELETE this file. It is the only definition of the DeltaComparison component and after the index.tsx edits above it has zero importers. (If the apply tooling cannot delete, replace the file body with an empty module `export {}` — but deletion is preferred; run `git rm web/src/components/f1-chat/charts/delta-comparison.tsx`.)

spec.verification_prompt: Donut (live routing): fire "How many sessions of each type were there in 2025?" through the chat UI — expect the deterministic session_type_share template to run, return `label`+`session_count` rows, and render a DonutChart (5-ish slices: Practice, Qualifying, Race, Sprint, Sprint Qualifying) with a "<total>\ntotal" center label. Delta_comparison + timeline deletion: run `cd web && npx tsc --noEmit` (or the project's `/mock typecheck`) and confirm zero errors after removing the two ChartType members, the delta-comparison.tsx file, and migrating the M15 fixture — then load the M15 fixture (incidentsMock) via /mock and confirm the penalty event timeline still renders identically via event_timeline → TimelineChart.

findings: BLOCKING (missed test-harness update): scripts/tests/template-router-topic-guards.test.mjs is the ONE test that transpiles+imports the REAL src/lib/deterministicSql.ts (files list line 45; imports deterministicSql.mjs at line 87). Its import-rewrite rules (lines 57-84, incl. the catch-all groups at lines 67 & 81) enumerate every deterministicSql/* module by name but do NOT include `sessionTypeShare`. After EDIT 1 adds `import { buildSessionTypeShareTemplate } from "./deterministicSql/sessionTypeShare"`, that import stays un-rewritten AND sessionTypeShare.mjs is never written to the temp dir, so `await import(deterministicSql.mjs)` throws module-not-found and EVERY test in this file fails in loadDeterministicSql(). Fix: add `sessionTypeShare.ts` to the harness `files` array (line ~44) AND add a rewrite rule mapping both `./sessionTypeShare` and `./deterministicSql/sessionTypeShare` to `./sessionTypeShare.mjs` (extend the group regexes on lines 67/81). The spec's verification_prompt only runs `tsc --noEmit` + a UI smoke, so it would NOT catch this. (The sibling stub-based harnesses — zero-llm, answer-cache, skip-repair, streaming-synthesis, all validators — use deterministicSql.stub.mjs and are unaffected; template-router-topic-coverage.test.mjs only text-scans and is unaffected.)

FACTUAL ERROR in the exempt-list rationale (non-blocking): the spec's topicGuards.ts edit claims `Without this, guardedReturn would reject the template because templateAllowsTopic returns false when no owned flag is present.` That is wrong. templateAllowsTopic (topicGuards.ts:520-521) does `const entry = TEMPLATE_TOPICS[templateKey]; if (!entry) return true;` — an UNKNOWN key returns TRUE, so guardedReturn would pass session_type_share regardless of the exempt list. The exempt list is consulted only by the coverage test's `exempt` set (template-router-topic-coverage.test.mjs:74), and that test won't even see the new key because sessionTypeShare.ts is absent from its SCAN_FILES (lines 18-43). The exempt edit is harmless/defensible but its stated justification is false and it is not strictly required.

INCOMPLETE reference sweep for delta_comparison (non-blocking, does not break typecheck): the spec asserts only the ChartType member, index.tsx (import/case/comment/re-export), and the component reference it. Two more mentions exist: (a) src/__mocks__/insights/manifest.ts:111 `renderer: "GroupedBarChart (DeltaComparison variant)"` — a descriptive string in m05's renderer field, not the ChartType union, so it is not typechecked (safe to leave, or retitle for tidiness); (b) src/lib/mapInsight/detectors/registry.ts:143 a code COMMENT `these belong to a delta_comparison chart` (safe). Neither breaks the build, but the spec's contract_risks claim that it removed `all delta_comparison references` is inaccurate.

spec.approach: Stop dropping all-zero axes in the radar detector. Retain every axis in `chart.axes` (full column set) and every series value, and add a new `empty_axes: string[]` field naming the axes that are exactly 0 across every series. Keep the existing `partial_data_axes`/`total_axes` count and the `<3 populated axes → grouped_bar` fallback (bars now show the full labelled axis set with an `empty_axes` marker). In radar-chart.tsx, grey + dash the PolarAngleAxis tick labels for empty axes via a custom tick renderer, keep the polygon vertices (they sit at 0 = center, honest), and render a small n/a readout listing the greyed axes with the preserved "insufficient to rank" messaging.
spec.verification_prompt: In the live chat UI, fire a 7-axis driver performance prompt where some axes are known-unpopulated, e.g. "Break down Verstappen vs Norris across all seven performance axes in 2025" — confirm the radar keeps all 7 spokes, the unpopulated ones are greyed/italic with ' · n/a', and the footer reads "⚠ N of 7 axes insufficient to rank" plus the "greyed (n/a): ..." list. Also run `node --test web/scripts/tests/wave4-visual-routing.test.mjs` to confirm the F13 season_year test still passes (radar retains real axes, drops off-scale).
spec.routing_note: No routing change: the detector `matches()` (column-shape: driver_name + ≥4 numeric or ≥3 *_axis cols) is untouched, so the same rows still route to `radar` at priority 88. The build() change only affects the emitted spec (retaining axes + adding empty_axes), not routing. The <3-populated-axes grouped_bar fallback path is preserved and now also carries empty_axes. The F13 wave4 test rows (4 populated axes, none all-zero) still yield type radar with no season/year axis, so that assertion holds.
spec.contract_risks: ChartSeries fields untouched (series passed through with name/values/color intact). Two-layer color rule preserved: team hues still come from getDistinctTeamStyles/getTeamColor via ChartSeries.color; the new greyed spoke label uses THEME tokens hsl(var(--muted-foreground)) + text-semantic-warning, not domain hex. Honesty UI preserved and strengthened (radar now shows retained empty axes instead of dropping). Typecheck risk: the new `empty_axes?: string[]` field is additive/optional so no existing consumer breaks; the custom PolarAngleAxis tick renderer is typed with an inline props interface (Recharts accepts a ReactElement for `tick`). One minor lint note: original imported `Legend` unused — my new file drops it; if any other file relies on that being present it does not (import is module-local).

findings: chart-types.ts OLD-string is wrong: the spec's OLD block ends with a trailing blank line and its UNIQUE-ANCHOR NOTE explicitly asserts 'match includes the trailing blank line before series?:'. In the real file (lines 114-116) there is NO blank line — line 115 'total_axes?: number;' is immediately followed by line 116 'series?: ChartSeries[];'. An exact-match Edit using the OLD string as written will FAIL. Fix: drop the phantom trailing blank line from the OLD anchor (the two non-blank lines are present and unique, so the match works once the blank line is removed).

Approach overstates the grouped_bar fallback: the spec claims 'bars now show the full labelled axis set with an empty_axes marker,' but grouped-bar-chart.tsx (web/src/components/f1-chat/charts/grouped-bar-chart.tsx) never reads chart.empty_axes — it renders only x_axis/series. The fallback path adds empty_axes to the spec, but it is silently ignored on the bar renderer; no visual 'marker' appears. Not a break (field is optional), but the stated honesty improvement does not reach the <3-live-axes fallback. Either drop the claim or add empty-axis handling to GroupedBarChart if that surface matters.

Cosmetic redundancy in the new tick renderer (not blocking): fill is set to the same value in both branches (isEmpty ? 'hsl(var(--muted-foreground))' : 'hsl(var(--muted-foreground))'); differentiation relies solely on fillOpacity/italic/' · n/a'. Harmless but pointless ternary — consider a real greyer token or just fillOpacity.

Not a defect, but a documented over-fire edge: applyCornerMap does NOT check insight.chart, so a single-corner query that also carries driver_name (e.g. 'Verstappen's entry/apex/exit speed through Turn 22') routes to the grouped_bar detector (registry.ts:161 returns true on corner_label + abs-speed col + driver_name) AND gets a corner_map. insight-card.tsx renders cornerMap (line 185) and chart (line 196) independently, so the card shows a 1-group bar chart AND the mini-map together. The corner_map is still accurate (single distinct corner), so this is cosmetic, not a correctness/contract break — but the spec's stated intent ('single-corner metric card') silently also applies to single-corner charts. The verification prompt's positive case (aggregate, no driver_name) avoids this since grouped_bar's matches() returns false without driver_name or >=2 wide prefixes.

Minor verification-command path nuance: spec says `tsx --test web/scripts/tests/mapInsight.test.ts` but the test header documents running from web/ as `tsx --test scripts/tests/mapInsight.test.ts`. The file is .ts (confirmed) and tsx is the correct runner; just adjust cwd/path.

TYPECHECK BREAK (P0): buildCornerDeltaInsight in the new web/src/lib/synthesis/cornerDeltaInsight.ts sets verdict.label to `"EVEN"` or `${overallLeader.toUpperCase()} EDGE`, but InsightFields["verdict"].label is the literal union `"YES" | "NO"` (web/src/lib/chatTypes.ts:111 AND web/src/components/f1-chat/insight-card.tsx:32). `npx tsc --noEmit` will fail. The spec's NOTE that 'brakeZonesInsight uses the same pattern, so known-good' is FALSE — brakeZonesInsight.ts only ever assigns label:"YES"/"NO" (a genuine yes/no foreshadow question). A5 is not a yes/no question, so the verdict slot is the wrong surface both by type and by intent (VerdictCard renders it as a big YES/NO bypass badge). FIX: drop `verdict` from the returned InsightFields and lead with `at_a_glance` (free-form string, exists on InsightFields) or fold the summary into key_takeaways/metrics.

MINOR (cosmetic, not a break): the ladder in the corner_delta_grid spec passes diverging_colors {positive:aColor, negative:bColor}, but DivergingBarChart (web/src/components/f1-chat/charts/diverging-bar-chart.tsx:31) colors each bar via getTeamColorByDriver(label) where label is a CORNER name (e.g. 'Turn 1'), NOT a driver — so per-bar team colors won't apply and bars fall back to a default hue. diverging_colors is only used as the domain fallback, not per-bar. The ladder will still render (signed values, +/- axis) but won't be team-colored as the spec narrative implies. Non-blocking; note for expectation-setting.

MINOR (global behavioral change): the grouped-bar-chart.tsx baseline-at-0 edit (domainMin: 0 for range>=10) affects EVERY residual grouped_bar with a large value range, not just absolute-speed charts. For most absolute magnitudes this is the honest choice, but any grouped_bar whose values legitimately sit far from 0 (large counts, etc.) will now waste vertical space. Acceptable per the anti-exaggeration rationale, but it is a global change beyond A5's own card — flag for regression-eyeballing residual grouped_bar cards.

Missing edit strings: the spec's only files entry says 'See files array above for the three exact old→new edits' but NO actual old→new find/replace strings (nor full-file contents) are present in the payload. The task requires mechanically-applicable EXACT edits; as delivered this cannot be applied. The three edits (isSelfTitle + titleFromRows insertion before line 494, applyQuestionTitle rewrite at 515-518, __test extension at 521-528) are only described in prose, never given as literal strings.

Unverifiable unit assertion: titleFromRows([{driver_name:'Lewis HAMILTON', circuit_short_name:'Silverstone', corner_label:'Turn 3'}], 'apex speed for Hamilton at Silverstone') === 'Apex Speed: HAMILTON — Silverstone Turn 3' presumes surname extraction from 'Lewis HAMILTON', a title-cased metric/topic hint ('Apex Speed') derived from the QUESTION not the rows, and a specific ':'/'—' join scheme — none of which is specified. The exact-string assertion cannot be checked without the actual titleFromRows body.

Minor: isSelfTitle example compares title 'Entry, apex, and exit speeds...' (with comma) vs question 'entry, apex and exit speeds...' (no 'and' comma) — equality requires a punctuation-stripping normalizer that is asserted but not defined in the provided spec text.

No routing/typecheck/contract issues found — spec is applicable as written. Verified: orchestration.ts:698-711 returns the FULL `runtime` object on the runtime_clarification branch, and runtime.resolution.sessionCandidates (chatRuntime.ts:106, 2334) is populated with the exact fields the mapper reads — sessionKey, sessionName, year, confidence, score, label (buildSessionLabel in chatRuntime/resolution.ts:202 joins with ' / ', matching the mapper's split('/')). So the data really is on the wire; only the ChatApiResponse type omits sessionCandidates today (chatTypes.ts:136-144 has no such field). The frontend re-send path is sound: parseSessionKeyMention (chatRuntime.ts:694-695) uses /\bsession(?:\s+key)?\s*(\d{3,6})\b/i, which '(session <key>)' matches.

No SQL/matview/detector change needed — correctly claimed false. This is a SLOT-style branch keyed on generationSource==='runtime_clarification', not a column-shape detector, so registry.ts is untouched. applyClarification early-returns unless generationSource==='runtime_clarification' AND candidates.length>=2, preserving prose/driver-pair clarifications and the honesty UI.

All OLD anchor strings verified present and unique: chatTypes.ts:136-144 (resolution block), chart-types.ts:223 corner_map + interface close (DraftInsight extends Omit<InsightMock,'title'> at :237 so the field propagates), mapInsight.ts:157-162 (applyResponseSemantics close + applyScalarHero banner; ChatApiResponse/DraftInsight/generationSource already imported at :1-2/:136), insight-card.tsx:5/23-24/71-73/151-152 (all three edits), toCardProps.ts:72-73, page.tsx:16-23 import block, :273-275 fold sequence, :397 InsightCard render.

Render/order edge cases check out. body='' + chart=undefined set by applyClarification cause no blank card: applyScalarHero (mapInsight.ts:203-205) and applyVerdictSemantics early-return because a clarification response has no result/rows (mapChatResponse.ts:25 only emits a table part when data.result exists), applyResponseSemantics (mapInsight.ts:138) only acts on no_data_refusal/proprietary sources, applyQuestionTitle still sets a title, and hasContent (insight-card.tsx:91) only gates the streaming 'Thinking…' fallback (streaming is false post-fold), NOT CardContent — the clarification block at :151 renders unconditionally above the hero. The duplicated prose is correctly cleared by body=''.

Token/contract rules preserved. All utility classes used by ClarificationCard resolve: text-red-text/bg-surface-raised/text-section-label registered in tailwind.config.ts:43/45/48; primary/border/muted-foreground/foreground are standard tokens. No domain hex, no ChartSeries.color touched → two-layer color rule intact. cn exported from utils.ts:4. Typecheck additive: new fields optional; the two structurally-identical ClarificationOption declarations (clarification-card.tsx vs chart-types.ts) are assignable; 'label ?? [...].join(" ") ?? "session"' compiles (join always yields string) — only a harmless redundant-nullish lint smell, not an error. `text` is in scope in handleSend (page.tsx:101).

BLOCKING (missed test-harness update): scripts/tests/template-router-topic-guards.test.mjs is the ONE test that transpiles+imports the REAL src/lib/deterministicSql.ts (files list line 45; imports deterministicSql.mjs at line 87). Its import-rewrite rules (lines 57-84, incl. the catch-all groups at lines 67 & 81) enumerate every deterministicSql/* module by name but do NOT include `sessionTypeShare`. After EDIT 1 adds `import { buildSessionTypeShareTemplate } from "./deterministicSql/sessionTypeShare"`, that import stays un-rewritten AND sessionTypeShare.mjs is never written to the temp dir, so `await import(deterministicSql.mjs)` throws module-not-found and EVERY test in this file fails in loadDeterministicSql(). Fix: add `sessionTypeShare.ts` to the harness `files` array (line ~44) AND add a rewrite rule mapping both `./sessionTypeShare` and `./deterministicSql/sessionTypeShare` to `./sessionTypeShare.mjs` (extend the group regexes on lines 67/81). The spec's verification_prompt only runs `tsc --noEmit` + a UI smoke, so it would NOT catch this. (The sibling stub-based harnesses — zero-llm, answer-cache, skip-repair, streaming-synthesis, all validators — use deterministicSql.stub.mjs and are unaffected; template-router-topic-coverage.test.mjs only text-scans and is unaffected.)

FACTUAL ERROR in the exempt-list rationale (non-blocking): the spec's topicGuards.ts edit claims `Without this, guardedReturn would reject the template because templateAllowsTopic returns false when no owned flag is present.` That is wrong. templateAllowsTopic (topicGuards.ts:520-521) does `const entry = TEMPLATE_TOPICS[templateKey]; if (!entry) return true;` — an UNKNOWN key returns TRUE, so guardedReturn would pass session_type_share regardless of the exempt list. The exempt list is consulted only by the coverage test's `exempt` set (template-router-topic-coverage.test.mjs:74), and that test won't even see the new key because sessionTypeShare.ts is absent from its SCAN_FILES (lines 18-43). The exempt edit is harmless/defensible but its stated justification is false and it is not strictly required.

INCOMPLETE reference sweep for delta_comparison (non-blocking, does not break typecheck): the spec asserts only the ChartType member, index.tsx (import/case/comment/re-export), and the component reference it. Two more mentions exist: (a) src/__mocks__/insights/manifest.ts:111 `renderer: "GroupedBarChart (DeltaComparison variant)"` — a descriptive string in m05's renderer field, not the ChartType union, so it is not typechecked (safe to leave, or retitle for tidiness); (b) src/lib/mapInsight/detectors/registry.ts:143 a code COMMENT `these belong to a delta_comparison chart` (safe). Neither breaks the build, but the spec's contract_risks claim that it removed `all delta_comparison references` is inaccurate.

chart-types.ts OLD-string is wrong: the spec's OLD block ends with a trailing blank line and its UNIQUE-ANCHOR NOTE explicitly asserts 'match includes the trailing blank line before series?:'. In the real file (lines 114-116) there is NO blank line — line 115 'total_axes?: number;' is immediately followed by line 116 'series?: ChartSeries[];'. An exact-match Edit using the OLD string as written will FAIL. Fix: drop the phantom trailing blank line from the OLD anchor (the two non-blank lines are present and unique, so the match works once the blank line is removed).

Approach overstates the grouped_bar fallback: the spec claims 'bars now show the full labelled axis set with an empty_axes marker,' but grouped-bar-chart.tsx (web/src/components/f1-chat/charts/grouped-bar-chart.tsx) never reads chart.empty_axes — it renders only x_axis/series. The fallback path adds empty_axes to the spec, but it is silently ignored on the bar renderer; no visual 'marker' appears. Not a break (field is optional), but the stated honesty improvement does not reach the <3-live-axes fallback. Either drop the claim or add empty-axis handling to GroupedBarChart if that surface matters.

Cosmetic redundancy in the new tick renderer (not blocking): fill is set to the same value in both branches (isEmpty ? 'hsl(var(--muted-foreground))' : 'hsl(var(--muted-foreground))'); differentiation relies solely on fillOpacity/italic/' · n/a'. Harmless but pointless ternary — consider a real greyer token or just fillOpacity.
